---
title: "MEE Sequence Analysis R code - cleaned & annotated"
output: html_notebook
---

##### Sexing Data Analysis #####
```{r}
# ============================================================
# Input:
#   eDNA_tissue_sexing_data.csv
# Output object:
#   sexing  (cleaned column names + standardized eDNA column names)
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(readr)

sexing <- readr::read_csv(
  "eDNA_tissue_sexing_data.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  rename(
    edna_sample = e_dna_sample,
    edna_sex    = e_dna_sex
  )

glimpse(sexing)
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# SEXING SUCCESS MODEL
# Logistic regression testing whether MarVer concentration
#     predicts successful molecular sex identification from eDNA
#
# Input:
#   sexing  (cleaned dataset from previous setup chunk)
#
# Steps:
#   1. Keep rows corresponding to eDNA samples
#   2. Define binary success variable (1 = sex assigned, 0 = no call)
#   3. Fit binomial GLM: success ~ marver_conc
# ============================================================

sexing_edna <- sexing %>%
  filter(!is.na(edna_sample)) %>%      # retain eDNA samples only
  mutate(success = if_else(!is.na(edna_sex), 1, 0))

model1 <- glm(
  success ~ marver_conc,
  data = sexing_edna,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model1)
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 3b
# Logistic regression visualization:
#     Probability of successful sex ID vs MarVer concentration
#
# Plot elements:
#   points  : observed outcomes (0 = failure, 1 = success)
#   curve   : binomial GLM fit (success ~ marver_conc)
#   shading : 95% confidence interval
# ============================================================

library(ggplot2)

p_sex_success <- ggplot(sexing_edna, aes(x = marver_conc, y = success)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.05, width = 0) +
  stat_smooth(
    method = "glm",
    method.args = list(family = "binomial"),
    se = TRUE,
    color = "orchid",
    fill  = "grey80"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Marine vertebrate DNA concentration (ng/" * mu * "L)"),
    y = "Predicted probability of sexing success"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p_sex_success
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUPPORTING FIGURE 3
# Spatial distribution of sexed eDNA samples
#
# Points show sampling locations colored by whether a
# sex ID was successfully recovered from the eDNA sample
# Point size represents MarVer concentration
#
#
# Variables used:
#   lon, lat        : sampling coordinates
#   success         : binary sexing outcome (0 = fail, 1 = success)
#   marver_conc     : Marine vertebrate DNA concentration
# ============================================================

p_sexing_map <- ggplot(sexing_edna, aes(x = lon, y = lat)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      color = factor(success),
      size  = marver_conc
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("grey60", "red3"),
    labels = c("0" = "FALSE", "1" = "TRUE")
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    color = "eDNA sex recovered",
    size  = "MarVer concentration (ng/µL)",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  )

p_sexing_map

```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SEX ASSIGNMENT CONCORDANCE
# Agreement between eDNA-derived sex ID and tissue-based sex
#
#
# Steps:
#   1. Keep samples where both tissue and eDNA sex were successfully called
#   2. Count total comparable samples
#   3. Count matches between tissue and eDNA sex
#   4. Compute concordance proportion
# ============================================================

sexing_called <- sexing %>%
  filter(!is.na(edna_sex),
         !is.na(tissue_sex))

n_called <- nrow(sexing_called)

n_match <- sum(
  tolower(sexing_called$edna_sex) ==
  tolower(sexing_called$tissue_sex)
)

concordance <- n_match / n_called

concordance
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# SEX ASSIGNMENT STATISTICAL TEST
# Binomial proportion test for concordance between eDNA and tissue sex IDs
#
# Input:
#   n_match  : number of matching sex assignments
#   n_called : total comparable samples
#
# Also prints the contingency table of eDNA vs tissue sex ID
# ============================================================

# Binomial test of concordance proportion
prop.test(n_match, n_called)

# Contingency table of eDNA vs tissue sex assignments
table(
  sexing_called$edna_sex,
  sexing_called$tissue_sex
)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 3a
# Concordance between tissue-derived and eDNA-derived sex ID
#
# Each row represents a paired sample / Rows are ordered so that:
#   1) matching female assignments appear first
#   2) mismatches appear in the middle
#   3) matching male assignments appear last
# ============================================================

sex_plot <- sexing %>%
  filter(!is.na(edna_sex)) %>%
  mutate(
    eDNA   = tolower(edna_sex),
    Tissue = tolower(tissue_sex),
    match  = eDNA == Tissue,
    order_group = case_when(
      match & Tissue == "female" ~ 1,
      !match                     ~ 2,
      match & Tissue == "male"   ~ 3
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(order_group) %>%
  mutate(sample_id = row_number()) %>%
  select(sample_id, eDNA, Tissue) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(eDNA, Tissue),
    names_to = "sample_type",
    values_to = "sex"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sample_type = factor(sample_type, levels = c("eDNA", "Tissue")),
    sample_id   = factor(sample_id, levels = rev(unique(sample_id)))
  )

p_sex_tiles <- ggplot(sex_plot, aes(x = sample_type, y = sample_id, fill = sex)) +
  geom_tile(color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "female" = "#e78ac3",
      "male"   = "#4ea3de"
    ),
    na.value = "white"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Tissue" = "tissue")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6)
  ) +
  labs(fill = "Sex")

p_sex_tiles

```

##### Nanopore Data Analysis #####
```{r}
# ============================================================
# Tier-3 SNP filtering (variant-only SNP tables) + REF fill
# Profiles: strict / moderate / relaxed
#
# Inputs:
#   - nanopore_barcode_metadata.csv
#   - snp_tables_medaka_*_d10000/*_snps.tsv   (variant-only SNP tables per barcode)
#
# Outputs (objects):
#   - profiles         : list of profile parameters
#   - qc_current           : Tier-1 QC metadata (barcode + sample_type + mapped_primary_reads)
#   - profile_results  : results per profile (snps, tier3 panel, hapmat, etc.)
#   - summary_tbl      : per-profile summary (Tier-1 counts, Tier-2 union, Tier-3 panel size)
#
# Optional written outputs (if save_outputs=TRUE):
#   - tier3_<profile>_panel.tsv
#   - tier3_<profile>_hapmat.tsv
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)

# ----------------------------
# 1) Profile parameters
# ----------------------------
profiles <- list(
  strict = list(
    dir = "snp_tables_medaka_strict_d10000",
    tier1_min_reads = 1000,
    vaf_low = 0.70, frac_low_max = 0.60, maf_min = 0.25,
    min_alt_hi = 2, alt_support_min = 2
  ),
  moderate = list(
    dir = "snp_tables_medaka_moderate_d10000",
    tier1_min_reads = 500,
    vaf_low = 0.65, frac_low_max = 0.70, maf_min = 0.20,
    min_alt_hi = 2, alt_support_min = 2
  ),
  relaxed = list(
    dir = "snp_tables_medaka_relaxed_d10000",
    tier1_min_reads = 250,
    vaf_low = 0.60, frac_low_max = 0.80, maf_min = 0.15,
    min_alt_hi = 2, alt_support_min = 2
  )
)

# ----------------------------
# 2) Tier-1 QC
# ----------------------------
qc_current <- readr::read_csv("nanopore_barcode_metadata.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(barcode = as.character(barcode))

stopifnot(all(c("barcode", "sample_type", "mapped_primary_reads") %in% names(qc_current)))

# ============================================================
# Function: read_profile_snps()
# Reads all *_snps.tsv files in a directory (variant-only tables)
# Produces a long table with DP4 parsed into rf/rr/af/ar + alt_count + vaf
# ============================================================
read_profile_snps <- function(dir, keep_barcodes = NULL) {

  files <- list.files(dir, pattern = "_snps\\.tsv$", full.names = TRUE)
  if (length(files) == 0) stop("No *_snps.tsv files found in: ", dir)

  read_one <- function(path) {

    bc <- stringr::str_remove(basename(path), "_snps\\.tsv$")

    # Optionally skip barcodes not in keep list
    if (!is.null(keep_barcodes) && !(bc %in% keep_barcodes)) return(NULL)

    x <- readr::read_tsv(
      path,
      col_names = FALSE,
      col_types = cols(
        X1 = col_character(),  # CHROM
        X2 = col_double(),     # POS
        X3 = col_character(),  # REF
        X4 = col_character(),  # ALT
        X5 = col_double(),     # QUAL
        X6 = col_double(),     # DP
        X7 = col_character()   # DP4
      ),
      show_col_types = FALSE,
      progress = FALSE
    )

    names(x) <- c("CHROM","POS","REF","ALT","QUAL","DP","DP4")

    x %>%
      mutate(
        barcode = bc,
        DP4 = dplyr::coalesce(DP4, ""),
        DP4 = stringr::str_trim(DP4),
        DP4 = stringr::str_replace(DP4, ",+$", "")
      )
  }

  snps <- purrr::map_dfr(files, read_one)

  snps %>%
    mutate(
      dp4 = stringr::str_split(DP4, ",", simplify = TRUE),
      rf = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dp4[, 1])),
      rr = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dp4[, 2])),
      af = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dp4[, 3])),
      ar = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dp4[, 4])),
      alt_count = dplyr::coalesce(af, 0) + dplyr::coalesce(ar, 0),
      vaf = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(DP) & DP > 0, alt_count / DP, NA_real_)
    ) %>%
    select(barcode, CHROM, POS, REF, ALT, QUAL, DP, rf, rr, af, ar, alt_count, vaf)
}

# ============================================================
# Function: tier3_define_panel_variant_only()
# Defines the Tier-3 "panel" of positions using variant-only tables
# ============================================================
tier3_define_panel_variant_only <- function(snps,
                                            barcodes_used,
                                            vaf_low,
                                            frac_low_max,
                                            maf_min,
                                            min_alt_hi,
                                            alt_support_min) {

  all_barcodes <- sort(unique(barcodes_used))

  snps <- snps %>% filter(barcode %in% all_barcodes)

  # "low_flag" for a barcode-position call:
  # - missing vaf
  # - below vaf threshold
  # - alt count below min_alt_hi
  snps2 <- snps %>%
    mutate(low_flag = is.na(vaf) | (vaf < vaf_low) | (alt_count < min_alt_hi))

  pos_low <- snps2 %>%
    group_by(CHROM, POS) %>%
    summarise(
      n_var_rows = n_distinct(barcode),
      n_low_var  = n_distinct(barcode[low_flag]),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      n_missing = length(all_barcodes) - n_var_rows,
      frac_low  = (n_low_var + n_missing) / length(all_barcodes)
    )

  pos_low_keep <- pos_low %>% filter(frac_low <= frac_low_max)

  # Alt-present indicator (only for non-low calls meeting vaf threshold)
  alt_present <- snps2 %>%
    filter(!low_flag, vaf >= vaf_low) %>%
    distinct(CHROM, POS, barcode) %>%
    mutate(alt_present = 1L)

  # MAF table: expand all CHROM x POS combinations seen in snps2
  maf_tbl_all <- tidyr::expand_grid(
    CHROM = sort(unique(snps2$CHROM)),
    POS   = sort(unique(snps2$POS)),
    barcode = all_barcodes
  ) %>%
    left_join(alt_present, by = c("CHROM","POS","barcode")) %>%
    mutate(alt_present = tidyr::replace_na(alt_present, 0L)) %>%
    group_by(CHROM, POS) %>%
    summarise(
      p_alt = mean(alt_present),
      maf   = pmin(p_alt, 1 - p_alt),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  maf_keep <- maf_tbl_all %>% filter(maf >= maf_min)

  # How many barcodes support ALT at each position (after low filtering) ?? 
  alt_support_all <- snps2 %>%
    filter(!low_flag, vaf >= vaf_low) %>%
    group_by(CHROM, POS) %>%
    summarise(alt_support = n_distinct(barcode), .groups = "drop")

  alt_keep <- alt_support_all %>% filter(alt_support >= alt_support_min)

  # Final Tier-3 panel = intersection of (frac_low) ∩ (MAF) ∩ (ALT support)
  panel <- pos_low_keep %>%
    inner_join(maf_keep, by = c("CHROM","POS")) %>%
    inner_join(alt_keep, by = c("CHROM","POS")) %>%
    arrange(POS)

  # Diagnostics table 
  diag <- tibble(
    step = c(
      "positions_total",
      paste0("frac_low <= ", frac_low_max),
      paste0("MAF >= ", maf_min),
      paste0("ALT_support >= ", alt_support_min),
      "final_intersection"
    ),
    n_positions = c(
      dplyr::n_distinct(snps2$POS),
      nrow(pos_low_keep),
      nrow(maf_keep),
      nrow(alt_keep),
      nrow(panel)
    )
  )

  list(
    panel = panel,
    diag = diag,
    pos_low_all = pos_low,
    maf_tbl_all = maf_tbl_all,
    alt_support_all = alt_support_all
  )
}

# ============================================================
# Function: make_hapmat_REFfill()
# Creates a haplotype matrix across Tier-3 positions
# Variant-only rows are used; missing positions are REF-filled using REF_base per position
# ============================================================
make_hapmat_REFfill <- function(snps, panel_tbl, barcodes_used, vaf_low, min_alt_hi) {

  panel_pos <- panel_tbl %>%
    transmute(CHROM, POS) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    arrange(POS)

  stopifnot(nrow(panel_pos) > 0)

  all_barcodes <- sort(unique(barcodes_used))

  # Determine REF base at each panel position from SNP table
  ref_by_pos <- snps %>%
    semi_join(panel_pos, by = c("CHROM","POS")) %>%
    group_by(CHROM, POS) %>%
    summarise(REF_base = dplyr::first(REF), .groups = "drop")

  # Calls at panel positions: ALT if not low and meets threshold, else REF
  calls <- snps %>%
    filter(barcode %in% all_barcodes) %>%
    semi_join(panel_pos, by = c("CHROM","POS")) %>%
    mutate(
      low_flag = is.na(vaf) | (vaf < vaf_low) | (alt_count < min_alt_hi),
      allele = dplyr::if_else(!low_flag & vaf >= vaf_low, ALT, REF)
    ) %>%
    select(barcode, CHROM, POS, allele)

  # Full grid across all barcodes x all panel positions
  grid <- tidyr::expand_grid(
    barcode = all_barcodes,
    panel_pos
  )

  # Merge calls into grid; fill missing allele with REF_base
  hap_long <- grid %>%
    left_join(calls, by = c("barcode","CHROM","POS")) %>%
    left_join(ref_by_pos, by = c("CHROM","POS")) %>%
    mutate(base = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(allele), allele, REF_base)) %>%
    mutate(POS = as.character(POS)) %>%
    select(barcode, POS, base)

  # Wide matrix: one row per barcode, one column per POS
  hap_long %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = POS, values_from = base) %>%
    arrange(barcode)
}

# ============================================================
# Function: run_profile()
# Runs Tier-1 filtering + reads SNP tables + Tier-3 panel + hap matrix
#     Optionally writes tier3_<profile>_panel.tsv + tier3_<profile>_hapmat.tsv
# ============================================================
run_profile <- function(p, name, qc_current,
                        qc_reads_col = "mapped_primary_reads",
                        save_outputs = TRUE) {
  keep_barcodes <- qc_current %>%
    filter(sample_type %in% c("tissue","eDNA")) %>%
    filter(.data[[qc_reads_col]] >= p$tier1_min_reads) %>%
    pull(barcode) %>%
    unique()

  if (length(keep_barcodes) == 0) {
    stop(
      "No barcodes passed Tier-1 for profile ", name,
      " using ", qc_reads_col, " >= ", p$tier1_min_reads
    )
  }

  # Read SNPs for these barcodes only
  snps <- read_profile_snps(p$dir, keep_barcodes = keep_barcodes) %>%
    left_join(qc_current %>% select(barcode, sample_type), by = "barcode")

  # Tier-3 panel definition
  t3 <- tier3_define_panel_variant_only(
    snps,
    barcodes_used   = keep_barcodes,
    vaf_low         = p$vaf_low,
    frac_low_max    = p$frac_low_max,
    maf_min         = p$maf_min,
    min_alt_hi      = p$min_alt_hi,
    alt_support_min = p$alt_support_min
  )

  message("\nTier3 diagnostics for: ", name)
  print(t3$diag)

  if (nrow(t3$panel) == 0) {
    stop(
      "Tier-3 panel is empty for profile: ", name,
      "\nTry lowering maf_min or vaf_low slightly for this profile."
    )
  }

  # Haplotype matrix with REF fill
  hapmat <- make_hapmat_REFfill(
    snps, t3$panel,
    barcodes_used = keep_barcodes,
    vaf_low       = p$vaf_low,
    min_alt_hi    = p$min_alt_hi
  )

  message(
    "DONE: ", name,
    " | barcodes=", length(keep_barcodes),
    " | panel_n=", nrow(t3$panel),
    " | hapmat=", nrow(hapmat), "x", (ncol(hapmat) - 1)
  )

  # Optional 
  if (isTRUE(save_outputs)) {
    out_prefix <- paste0("tier3_", name)
    readr::write_tsv(t3$panel, paste0(out_prefix, "_panel.tsv"))
    readr::write_tsv(hapmat,   paste0(out_prefix, "_hapmat.tsv"))
  }

  list(
    name = name,
    params = p,
    barcodes_used = keep_barcodes,
    snps = snps,
    tier3 = t3,
    panel_pos = t3$panel$POS,
    hapmat = hapmat
  )
}

# ----------------------------
# 3) Run all profiles
# ----------------------------
profile_results <- purrr::imap(profiles, ~ run_profile(.x, .y, qc_current = qc_current))

# ----------------------------
# 4) Summary table (Tier-1 + Tier-2 union + Tier-3 final panel size)
# ----------------------------
summary_tbl <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, function(res, nm) {
  tibble(
    profile = nm,
    tier1_min_reads = res$params$tier1_min_reads,
    n_barcodes = length(res$barcodes_used),
    n_sites_tier2_union = dplyr::n_distinct(res$snps$POS),
    n_sites_final = nrow(res$tier3$panel)
  )
})

print(summary_tbl)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# TIER-3 DIAGNOSTICS SUMMARY (per profile)
# Extracts position counts at each Tier-3 filtering step from res$tier3$diag
#   plus the Tier-2 union of variant positions observed across barcodes
#
# Input:
#   - profile_results
#
# Output object:
#   - summary_tbl2    : per-profile diagnostics table
# ============================================================

summary_tbl2 <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, function(res, nm) {

  d <- res$tier3$diag

  tibble::tibble(
    profile = nm,
    n_barcodes = length(res$barcodes_used),
    tier2_union_pos = dplyr::n_distinct(res$snps$POS),
    after_frac_low  = d$n_positions[stringr::str_detect(d$step, "^frac_low")][1],
    after_maf       = d$n_positions[stringr::str_detect(d$step, "^MAF")][1],
    after_alt_sup   = d$n_positions[stringr::str_detect(d$step, "^ALT_support")][1],
    final_panel     = d$n_positions[d$step == "final_intersection"][1]
  )

})

print(summary_tbl2)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# PANEL RECOVERY SUMMARY (callable sites per barcode)
# Descriptive QC summarizing how many Tier-3 panel positions are callable
# per barcode, then averaging by sample_type
#
# Inputs:
#   - profile_results : list with $hapmat and $panel_pos per profile (from Tier-3 step)
#   - qc_current         : barcode metadata with sample_type
#
# Output object:
#   - panel_recovery_summary : per-profile x sample_type summary table
#
# Notes:
#   - Uses REF-filled hapmat / callable here is "not NA" in hapmat
#   - This is descriptive QC only / does not alter downstream inference
# ============================================================

panel_recovery_summary <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, function(res, profile_name) {

  hapmat  <- res$hapmat
  panel_n <- length(res$panel_pos)

  hapmat2 <- hapmat %>%
    left_join(qc_sub %>% select(barcode, sample_type), by = "barcode")

  # Count callable SNPs per barcode across Tier-3 panel
  per_barcode <- hapmat2 %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -c(barcode, sample_type),
      names_to = "POS",
      values_to = "base"
    ) %>%
    group_by(barcode, sample_type) %>%
    summarise(
      n_callable = sum(!is.na(base)),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(frac_callable = n_callable / panel_n)

  # Summarize callable recovery by sample_type
  per_barcode %>%
    group_by(sample_type) %>%
    summarise(
      profile            = profile_name,
      tier1_min_reads    = res$params$tier1_min_reads,
      panel_size         = panel_n,
      n_barcodes         = n(),
      mean_callable      = mean(n_callable),
      mean_frac_callable = mean(frac_callable),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

})

panel_recovery_summary

```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# DEPTH TABLES
# Build depth_long from per-barcode depth files
#
# Inputs:
#   - depth/ directory with *_clean.depth files (CHROM, POS, DP)
#   - qc_current: data frame with barcode-level metadata:
#       barcode, profile, sample_type, num_fragments
#
# Output object:
#   - depth_long : long table with columns
#       barcode, CHROM, POS, DP, profile, sample_type, num_fragments
#
# Notes:
#   - qc_current is collapsed to one row per barcode
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)

# ----------------------------
# 1) Read per-barcode depth files
# ----------------------------
depth_dir <- "depth"
depth_files <- list.files(depth_dir, pattern = "_clean\\.depth$", full.names = TRUE)

depth_long0 <- purrr::map_dfr(depth_files, \(f) {

  bc <- stringr::str_remove(basename(f), "_clean\\.depth$")

  readr::read_tsv(
    f,
    col_names = c("CHROM","POS","DP"),
    col_types = cols(
      CHROM = col_character(),
      POS   = col_double(),
      DP    = col_double()
    ),
    progress = FALSE,
    show_col_types = FALSE
  ) %>%
    mutate(barcode = bc)

})

# ----------------------------
# 2) One-row-per-barcode metadata (from qc_current)
# ----------------------------
names(qc_current)
meta_bp_1 <- qc_current %>%
  mutate(barcode = as.character(barcode)) %>%
  group_by(barcode) %>%
  summarise(
    profile       = dplyr::first(na.omit(profile)),
    sample_type   = dplyr::first(na.omit(sample_type)),
    num.fragments = dplyr::first(na.omit(num_fragments)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ----------------------------
# 3) Join metadata onto depth table
# ----------------------------
depth_long <- depth_long0 %>%
  mutate(barcode = as.character(barcode)) %>%
  left_join(meta_bp_1, by = "barcode")

```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# COVERAGE BREADTH SUMMARY FOR FIGURE 4 (cov_long)
# Computes per-barcode breadth of coverage at depth thresholds:
#   ≥1×, ≥10×, ≥50×, ≥100×
#
# Inputs:
#   - depth_long : long table with per-position depth
#   - qc_current : barcode metadata (barcode, sample_type, profile, num.fragments)
#
# Output:
#   - cov_long : long table used for Figure 4
# ============================================================

# Optional profile-only versions
# depth_use <- depth_long %>% filter(profile == "strict")
# depth_use <- depth_long %>% filter(profile == "moderate")
# depth_use <- depth_long %>% filter(profile == "relaxed")

depth_use <- depth_long

# Barcode -> sample_type mapping
sample_type_df <- qc_current %>%
  select(barcode, sample_type) %>%
  distinct()

# Per-barcode coverage breadth at each threshold
cov_stats <- depth_use %>%
  mutate(DP = as.numeric(DP)) %>%
  group_by(barcode) %>%
  summarise(
    cov_1x   = mean(DP >= 1,   na.rm = TRUE),
    cov_10x  = mean(DP >= 10,  na.rm = TRUE),
    cov_50x  = mean(DP >= 50,  na.rm = TRUE),
    cov_100x = mean(DP >= 100, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cov_plot_df <- cov_stats %>%
  left_join(sample_type_df, by = "barcode") %>%
  mutate(sample_type = factor(sample_type, levels = c("tissue","eDNA")))

# Long format for plotting
cov_long <- cov_plot_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("cov_"),
    names_to = "coverage_level",
    values_to = "proportion"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    coverage_level = factor(
      coverage_level,
      levels = c("cov_1x","cov_10x","cov_50x","cov_100x"),
      labels = c("≥1×","≥10×","≥50×","≥100×")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(sample_type %in% c("tissue","eDNA"))
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 4a
# Breadth of mitogenome coverage across depth thresholds
#     comparing tissue vs eDNA samples
#
# Required object:
#   cov_long
# ============================================================

p_cov_box <- ggplot(
  cov_long,
  aes(x = sample_type, y = proportion, fill = sample_type)
) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ coverage_level, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(tissue = "grey20", eDNA = "orchid2")) +
  labs(
    title = "Breadth of coverage comparison between tissue and eDNA samples",
    x = "",
    y = "Proportion of genome covered",
    fill = "Sample type"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

p_cov_box
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 4b
# Coverage decay curves per barcode across depth thresholds
# ============================================================

p_cov_decay <- ggplot(
  cov_long,
  aes(
    x = coverage_level,
    y = proportion,
    group = barcode,
    color = sample_type
  )
) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(tissue = "grey20", eDNA = "orchid2")) +
  labs(
    x = "Minimum depth threshold",
    y = "Proportion of mitogenome covered",
    color = "Sample type"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

p_cov_decay
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# ALT-SITE COUNTING PER BARCODE (within Tier-3 panel)
# For each profile:
#   - Compares REF-filled hapmat base calls to the REF base at each panel position
#   - Summarizes, per barcode:
#       n_panel_sites = number of panel positions evaluated
#       n_alt_sites   = number of positions where base != REF_base
#
# Inputs:
#   - profile_results : list from Tier-3 pipeline
#   - qc_current : barcode metadata with sample_type
#
# Output objects:
#   - barcode_snp_counts : per-barcode ALT-site counts for each profile
# ============================================================

barcode_alt_counts <- function(res, profile_name) {

  panel_pos <- res$tier3$panel %>%
    select(CHROM, POS) %>%
    distinct()

  # Reference base at each panel position
  ref_by_pos <- res$snps %>%
    semi_join(panel_pos, by = c("CHROM","POS")) %>%
    group_by(CHROM, POS) %>%
    summarise(REF_base = dplyr::first(REF), .groups = "drop")

  # hapmat -> long / compare base to reference base
  hap_long <- res$hapmat %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -barcode,
      names_to = "POS",
      values_to = "base"
    ) %>%
    mutate(POS = as.double(POS)) %>%
    left_join(ref_by_pos, by = "POS") %>%   # NOTE: join by POS only
    mutate(is_alt = !is.na(REF_base) & base != REF_base)

  hap_long %>%
    summarise(
      profile       = profile_name,
      n_panel_sites = dplyr::n_distinct(POS),
      n_alt_sites   = sum(is_alt, na.rm = TRUE),
      .by = barcode
    ) %>%
    left_join(qc_current %>% select(barcode, sample_type), by = "barcode")
}

barcode_snp_counts <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, barcode_alt_counts)

barcode_snp_counts %>%
  arrange(profile, sample_type, desc(n_alt_sites))
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUPPORTING FIGURE 4
# Breadth vs number of LR-PCR fragments amplified (tissue vs eDNA)
#
# Required object:
#   qc_current
#
# Outputs:
#   - p_supp4_breadth10_by_frags
#   - p_supp4_breadth1_by_frags
#   - p_supp4_breadth10_by_frags_profile
# ============================================================

# S4a: Breadth at ≥10× by fragments (qc_current)
p_supp4_breadth10_by_frags <- ggplot(
  qc_current,
  aes(x = factor(num_fragments), y = breadth_10x, fill = sample_type)
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(tissue = "lightgrey", eDNA = "orchid2")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Number of LR-PCR fragments amplified",
    y = "Breadth at ≥10× depth",
    fill = "Sample type"
  )

p_supp4_breadth10_by_frags

# S4b: Breadth at ≥1× by fragments (qc_current)
p_supp4_breadth1_by_frags <- ggplot(
  qc_current,
  aes(x = factor(num_fragments), y = breadth_1x, fill = sample_type)
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(tissue = "lightgrey", eDNA = "orchid2")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Number of LR-PCR fragments amplified",
    y = "Breadth at ≥1× depth",
    fill = "Sample type"
  )

p_supp4_breadth1_by_frags

# S4c: Breadth ≥10× by fragments, faceted by profile (qc_current)
p_supp4_breadth10_by_frags_profile <- ggplot(
  qc_current,
  aes(x = factor(num_fragments), y = breadth_10x, fill = sample_type)
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ profile) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("tissue" = "grey70", "eDNA" = "orchid2")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Fragments amplified",
    y = "Breadth at ≥10×",
    fill = "Sample type"
  )

p_supp4_breadth10_by_frags_profile
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUPPORTING FIGURE 5
# ALT sites (panel) vs breadth ≥10×, faceted by profile
#
# Required object:
#   qc_current 
#
# Output:
#   - p_supp5_alt_vs_cov
# ============================================================

p_supp5_alt_vs_cov <- ggplot(
  qc_current,
  aes(x = breadth_10x, y = n_alt_sites, color = sample_type)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ profile, nrow = 1) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Sample type",
    values = c("tissue" = "grey70", "eDNA" = "orchid2")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Breadth ≥10x",
    y = "ALT sites (panel)",
    fill = "Sample type"
  )

p_supp5_alt_vs_cov

```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# PANEL GENOMIC DISTRIBUTION (Tier-3 SNP positions)
#
# Builds a combined table of SNP positions from each profile's
#     panel and visualizes their genomic distribution
#
# Required object:
#   profile_results
#
# Output:
#   panel_pos_all : long table of panel SNP positions
#   p_panel_dist  : histogram plot of SNP distribution across genome
# ============================================================

prof_levels <- c("strict","moderate","relaxed")

panel_pos_all <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, \(res, profile_name) {
  res$tier3$panel %>%
    transmute(
      profile = profile_name,
      POS     = as.double(POS)
    )
}) %>%
  mutate(profile = factor(profile, levels = prof_levels))

p_panel_dist <- ggplot(panel_pos_all, aes(x = POS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60) +
  facet_wrap(~ profile, nrow = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Mitogenome position (bp)",
    y = "Number of panel SNPs",
    title = "Panel genomic distribution (Tier-3 SNP positions)"
  )

p_panel_dist
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# ALT-support by position, split by tissue vs eDNA
#
# Counts the number of barcodes supporting a high-confidence ALT
#   allele at each panel position, separated by sample type
#
# Required objects:
#   profile_results  : list of Tier-3 results for each profile
#   panel_pos_all    : table of Tier-3 panel SNP positions
#   prof_levels      : factor ordering for profiles
#
# Output:
#   alt_support_by_pos : ALT-support calls per barcode/position
#   alt_pos_counts     : number of ALT-supporting barcodes per position
# ============================================================

alt_support_by_pos <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, \(res, profile_name) {

  vaf_low    <- res$params$vaf_low
  min_alt_hi <- res$params$min_alt_hi

  res$snps %>%
    mutate(
      profile  = profile_name,
      POS      = as.double(POS),
      pass_alt = !is.na(vaf) & vaf >= vaf_low & alt_count >= min_alt_hi
    ) %>%
    filter(pass_alt) %>%
    semi_join(
      panel_pos_all %>% filter(profile == profile_name),
      by = c("profile","POS")
    ) %>%
    distinct(profile, POS, barcode, sample_type) %>%
    mutate(sample_type = factor(sample_type, levels = c("tissue","eDNA")))

}) %>%
  mutate(profile = factor(profile, levels = prof_levels))

# Count ALT-supporting barcodes per position
alt_pos_counts <- alt_support_by_pos %>%
  count(profile, sample_type, POS, name = "n_alt_barcodes")
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# BUILD alleles_panel + pos_support (used for Figure 5a)
#
# Goal:
#   1) Convert each profile's REF-filled hapmat into a long allele table
#   2) Attach REF_base per position and barcode sample_type (tissue/eDNA)
#   3) Restrict to Tier-3 panel positions -> alleles_panel
#   4) Summarize ALT support by position & sample_type -> pos_support
#
# Required objects:
#   - profile_results : list from run_profile() for strict/moderate/relaxed
#       each res contains $hapmat, $snps, $tier3$panel
#   - qc_current
#
# Output objects:
#   - alleles_panel : long allele table at Tier-3 panel positions
#       columns include: profile, barcode, POS, allele, sample_type, REF_base, is_alt
#   - pos_support   : ALT-support summary by profile x POS (tissue vs eDNA counts)
#       columns include: profile, POS, tissue, eDNA
# ============================================================

# ----------------------------
# Long allele table from hapmat (barcode + SNP columns)
# ----------------------------
hapmat_to_long <- function(hapmat, profile_name) {
  tibble::as_tibble(hapmat) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = -barcode,
      names_to = "POS",
      values_to = "allele"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      profile = profile_name,
      POS = as.double(POS),
      barcode = as.character(barcode)
    )
}

# ----------------------------
# 1) Long alleles from hapmat for all profiles
# ----------------------------
alleles_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, \(res, profile_name) {
  hapmat_to_long(res$hapmat, profile_name)
})

# ----------------------------
# 2) REF base per profile x POS (from SNP tables)
# ----------------------------
ref_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, \(res, profile_name) {
  res$snps %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      profile  = profile_name,
      POS      = as.double(POS),
      REF_base = as.character(REF)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
})

# ----------------------------
# 3) Barcode -> sample_type lookup (from qc_current)
# ----------------------------
bc_type <- qc_current %>%
  dplyr::select(profile, barcode, sample_type) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    barcode = as.character(barcode)
  )

# ----------------------------
# 4) Attach sample_type + REF_base / define is_alt
# ----------------------------
alleles_long2 <- alleles_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(profile = as.character(profile)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(bc_type,  by = c("profile","barcode")) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(ref_long, by = c("profile","POS")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    is_alt = !is.na(REF_base) & !is.na(allele) & allele != REF_base
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(sample_type))

# ----------------------------
# 5) panel positions per profile
# ----------------------------
panel_pos <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, \(res, profile_name) {
  res$tier3$panel %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      profile = profile_name,
      POS     = as.double(POS)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
})

# ----------------------------
# 6) Restrict to panel -> alleles_panel
# ----------------------------
alleles_panel <- alleles_long2 %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(panel_pos, by = c("profile","POS"))

# ----------------------------
# 7) ALT-support summary by profile x POS (counts of ALT calls in tissue vs eDNA)
# ----------------------------
pos_support <- alleles_panel %>%
  dplyr::group_by(profile, POS, sample_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_alt = sum(is_alt, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = sample_type,
    values_from = n_alt,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    tissue = if ("tissue" %in% names(.)) tissue else 0,
    eDNA   = if ("eDNA"   %in% names(.)) eDNA   else 0
  )

# cHECK
pos_support %>% summarise(
  n_positions   = dplyr::n(),
  n_edna_zero   = sum(eDNA == 0),
  n_tissue_zero = sum(tissue == 0)
)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 5a: Genomic positions of Tier-3 SNPs (shared vs tissue-only)
#
# Required object:
#   pos_support with columns:
#     - profile
#     - POS
#     - support_class
#
# Output:
#   - p_rug
# ============================================================

p_rug <- ggplot(pos_support, aes(x = POS, y = 0)) +

  # plot grey first (tissue-only)
  geom_point(
    data = dplyr::filter(pos_support, support_class == "tissue only"),
    aes(color = support_class),
    size = 1.6, alpha = 1
  ) +

  # green second (shared)
  geom_point(
    data = dplyr::filter(pos_support, support_class == "Shared (tissue + eDNA)"),
    aes(color = support_class),
    size = 1.6, alpha = 1
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~ profile, ncol = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 16500)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Shared (tissue + eDNA)" = "#008B45",
      "tissue only"            = "grey55"
    ),
    breaks = c("Shared (tissue + eDNA)", "tissue only")
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(x = "Mitogenome position (bp)", color = NULL)

p_rug
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 5b: ALT-support by mitogenome position within panel
# Required object:
#   - alt_pos_counts
# ============================================================

p_panel_dist_split <- ggplot(
  alt_pos_counts,
  aes(x = POS, weight = n_alt_barcodes, fill = sample_type)
) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, position = "identity", alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ profile, nrow = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "tissue" = "grey70",
      "eDNA"   = "orchid2"
    )
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Mitogenome position (bp)",
    y = "SNP support (sum of barcodes with ALT at site)",
    fill = "Sample type"
  )

p_panel_dist_split
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# CALLABILITY SUPPORT PER POSITION (callability_pos)
# Counts # barcodes with DP >= 10 at each POS, split by sample_type, per profile
#
# Required objects:
#   - depth_long  : columns barcode, POS, DP, profile, sample_type
#   - qc_current
#
# Output:
#   - callability_pos : columns profile, POS, sample_type, n_callable
# ============================================================

# Ensure depth_long has profile + sample_type (safe no-op if already present)
depth_long2 <- depth_long %>%
  mutate(
    barcode = as.character(barcode),
    POS     = as.double(POS),
    DP      = as.double(DP)
  ) %>%
  { if (all(c("profile","sample_type") %in% names(.))) . else
      left_join(
        qc_current %>%
          transmute(
            barcode     = as.character(barcode),
            profile     = as.character(profile),
            sample_type = as.character(sample_type)
          ) %>%
          distinct(),
        by = "barcode"
      )
  }

callability_pos <- depth_long2 %>%
  filter(sample_type %in% c("tissue","eDNA")) %>%
  mutate(callable10 = DP >= 10) %>%
  group_by(profile, POS, sample_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_callable = sum(callable10, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    profile     = factor(profile, levels = c("strict","moderate","relaxed")),
    sample_type = factor(sample_type, levels = c("tissue","eDNA"))
  )
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 5c INPUT: callability_pos (DP >= 10 across mitogenome)
# Proportion/callability proxy = # barcodes with DP >= 10 at each POS
#     Split by sample_type, faceted by profile
#
# Required objects:
#   - depth_long : long depth table from *_clean.depth
#       must include barcode, POS, DP
#   - qc_current
#
# Output:
#   - callability_pos : profile, POS, sample_type, n_callable
# ============================================================

prof_levels <- c("strict","moderate","relaxed")

depth_all <- depth_long %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    barcode = as.character(barcode),
    POS     = as.double(POS),
    DP      = as.double(DP)
  )

# Attach profile + sample_type from qc_current
depth_all <- depth_all %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    qc_current %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        barcode     = as.character(barcode),
        profile     = as.character(profile),
        sample_type = as.character(sample_type)
      ) %>%
      dplyr::distinct(),
    by = "barcode"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(sample_type %in% c("tissue","eDNA")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    profile     = factor(profile, levels = prof_levels),
    sample_type = factor(sample_type, levels = c("tissue","eDNA"))
  )

# Count callable barcodes per position
callability_pos <- depth_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(callable10 = DP >= 10) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(profile, POS, sample_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_callable = sum(callable10, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 5c: Callability support (DP ≥ 10) by position
# Required object:
#   - callability_pos: columns profile, sample_type, POS, n_callable
# ============================================================

p_callability_pos <- ggplot(
  callability_pos,
  aes(x = POS, y = n_callable, fill = sample_type)
) +
  geom_col(position = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ profile) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "tissue" = "grey80",
      "eDNA"   = "orchid2"
    )
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Mitogenome position (bp)",
    y = "Callability support\n(# barcodes with DP ≥ 10)",
    fill = "Sample type"
  )

p_callability_pos
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# Build conc_plot (SNP-level concordance table) for:
#   - Figure 6 tile heatmaps
#   - Supporting Figure 6 (>=80%)
#
# Required objects:
#   - alleles_panel: profile, barcode, POS, allele, sample_type
#   - paired_samples_subset.csv
#
# Output:
#   - pairs_one / pairs_tbl
#   - panel_index
#   - conc_plot: profile, tissue_barcode, edna_barcode, POS, tissue_allele, edna_allele,
#                status, snp_index, pair_id
# ============================================================

prof_levels <- c("strict","moderate","relaxed")

# 1) Read paired table
paired_samples <- readr::read_csv("paired_samples_subset.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    profile     = as.character(profile),
    sample_type = tolower(as.character(sample_type)),
    barcode     = as.character(barcode),
    sample_pair = dplyr::na_if(as.character(sample_pair), "NA")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(profile %in% prof_levels)

# 2) One row per true tissue–eDNA pair
pairs_one <- paired_samples %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(sample_pair)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    tissue_barcode = dplyr::case_when(
      sample_type == "tissue" ~ barcode,
      sample_type == "edna"   ~ sample_pair,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    edna_barcode = dplyr::case_when(
      sample_type == "edna"   ~ barcode,
      sample_type == "tissue" ~ sample_pair,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(tissue_barcode), !is.na(edna_barcode)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    pair_id = paste(tissue_barcode, "vs", edna_barcode),
    profile = factor(profile, levels = prof_levels) 
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(profile, pair_id, tissue_barcode, edna_barcode, .keep_all = TRUE)

pairs_one %>% dplyr::count(profile)

pairs_tbl <- pairs_one %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    tissue_barcode = as.character(tissue_barcode),
    edna_barcode   = as.character(edna_barcode),
    pair_id
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

# 3) Panel SNP index per profile (based on alleles_panel POS set)
panel_index <- alleles_panel %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    POS     = as.double(POS)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(profile, POS) %>%
  dplyr::filter(profile %in% prof_levels) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(factor(profile, levels = prof_levels), POS) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(profile) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(snp_index = dplyr::row_number()) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 4) Allele tables (tissue + eDNA) from alleles_panel
tissue_calls <- alleles_panel %>%
  dplyr::filter(sample_type == "tissue") %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    profile        = as.character(profile),
    tissue_barcode = as.character(barcode),
    POS            = as.double(POS),
    tissue_allele  = as.character(allele)
  )

edna_calls <- alleles_panel %>%
  dplyr::filter(sample_type == "eDNA") %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    profile      = as.character(profile),
    edna_barcode = as.character(barcode),
    POS          = as.double(POS),
    edna_allele  = as.character(allele)
  )

# 5) Build conc_plot (expand tissue SNPs / join eDNA alleles by POS)
conc_plot <- pairs_tbl %>%
  dplyr::left_join(tissue_calls, by = c("profile","tissue_barcode")) %>%          # expands to all tissue SNPs
  dplyr::left_join(edna_calls,   by = c("profile","edna_barcode","POS")) %>%      # adds eDNA allele if present
  dplyr::mutate(
    status = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(edna_allele) ~ "no eDNA call",
      edna_allele == tissue_allele ~ "match",
      TRUE ~ "mismatch"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(panel_index, by = c("profile","POS"))

# Check
conc_plot %>% dplyr::count(profile, status)

conc_plot %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    any_na_index = any(is.na(snp_index)),
    n_na_index   = sum(is.na(snp_index)),
    .by = profile
  )
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# Callable positions per barcode (DP >= 10)
#
# Required:
#   depth_long 
# 
# Output:
#   callable_by_barcode: barcode, POS, callable10
# ============================================================

callable_by_barcode <- depth_long0 %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    barcode = as.character(barcode),
    POS     = as.double(POS),
    DP      = as.numeric(DP),
    callable10 = DP >= 10
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(barcode, POS, callable10)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Callable mask for paired comparisons (DP >= 10 in BOTH barcodes)
# This expands each true pair to the panel POS for that profile
#   then checks DP>=10 for tissue and eDNA at that POS (profile-independent)
#
# Required:
#   - pairs_tbl: profile, tissue_barcode, edna_barcode, pair_id
#   - panel_pos_all: profile, POS   (Tier-3 positions per profile)
#   - callable_by_barcode: barcode, POS, callable10
#
# Output:
#   callable_pairs: profile, pair_id, POS, callable_both
# ============================================================

callable_pairs <- pairs_tbl %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    tissue_barcode = as.character(tissue_barcode),
    edna_barcode   = as.character(edna_barcode),
    pair_id        = as.character(pair_id)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    panel_pos_all %>%
      dplyr::mutate(profile = as.character(profile),
                    POS = as.double(POS)),
    by = "profile"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    callable_by_barcode %>%
      dplyr::rename(tissue_barcode = barcode, callable_tissue = callable10),
    by = c("tissue_barcode","POS")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    callable_by_barcode %>%
      dplyr::rename(edna_barcode = barcode, callable_edna = callable10),
    by = c("edna_barcode","POS")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    callable_tissue = dplyr::coalesce(callable_tissue, FALSE),
    callable_edna   = dplyr::coalesce(callable_edna,   FALSE),
    callable_both   = callable_tissue & callable_edna
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(profile, pair_id, POS, callable_both) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

# Check
callable_pairs %>% dplyr::count(profile, callable_both)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUPPORTING FIGURE 6 (callable-only)
# Uses conc_plot + callable_pairs to compute per-SNP agreement
#   among callable pairs only (DP>=10 in BOTH barcodes at that POS)
# ============================================================

pair_alleles2_new <- conc_plot %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    POS     = as.double(POS),
    snp_index = as.integer(snp_index),
    pair_id = paste(tissue_barcode, "vs", edna_barcode)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(callable_pairs, by = c("profile","pair_id","POS")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(callable_both = dplyr::coalesce(callable_both, FALSE)) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    profile,
    edna_barcode   = as.character(edna_barcode),
    tissue_barcode = as.character(tissue_barcode),
    POS,
    snp_index,
    allele_edna   = dplyr::na_if(as.character(edna_allele), "N"),
    allele_tissue = dplyr::na_if(as.character(tissue_allele), "N"),
    callable_both,
    pair_id
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    both_called = callable_both & !is.na(allele_edna) & !is.na(allele_tissue),
    match       = both_called & (allele_edna == allele_tissue)
  )

per_snp_agree_new <- pair_alleles2_new %>%
  dplyr::group_by(profile, POS, snp_index) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_pairs    = dplyr::n_distinct(pair_id),
    n_called   = sum(both_called, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_match    = sum(match, na.rm = TRUE),
    agree_rate = ifelse(n_called > 0, n_match / n_called, NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

diag_df2_new <- per_snp_agree_new %>%
  dplyr::group_by(profile) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(snp_index, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(shared = !is.na(agree_rate) & agree_rate >= 0.8) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

per_snp_agree_new %>%
  dplyr::group_by(profile) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    min_called = min(n_called, na.rm = TRUE),
    med_called = median(n_called, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_called = max(n_called, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_na_agree = sum(is.na(agree_rate)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

diag_df2_new <- per_snp_agree_new %>%
  mutate(shared = !is.na(agree_rate) & agree_rate >= 0.8)

diag_df2_new %>% count(profile, shared)

plot_diag_one2 <- function(df, prof) {
  d <- df %>% filter(profile == prof) %>% mutate(snp_index = as.integer(snp_index))
  stopifnot(nrow(d) > 0)

  max_i <- max(d$snp_index, na.rm = TRUE)

  ggplot(d, aes(x = snp_index, y = snp_index)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
    geom_point(data = d %>% filter(shared), size = 1.6) +
    coord_equal(xlim = c(1, max_i), ylim = c(1, max_i)) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(
      title = paste0(prof, " panel (", max_i, " SNPs)"),
      subtitle = "Dots = SNPs with ≥80% allele agreement across\npaired eDNA–tissue (among callable pairs)",
      x = "SNP",
      y = "SNP"
    )
}

p_strict2   <- plot_diag_one2(diag_df2_new, "strict")
p_moderate2 <- plot_diag_one2(diag_df2_new, "moderate")
p_relaxed2  <- plot_diag_one2(diag_df2_new, "relaxed")

p_strict2
p_moderate2
p_relaxed2
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# How many true tissue–eDNA pairs per profile?
#
# Required object:
#   - pairs_one
#
# Output:
#   - pairs_summary: n_pairs per profile
# ============================================================

pairs_summary <- pairs_one %>%
  dplyr::count(profile, name = "n_pairs")

pairs_summary
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# HAPLOTYPES + HAPLOGROUPS (pegas + ape)
# Required: 
#   - profile_results
#   - qc_current      
#   - pairs_one  
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ape)
library(pegas)

# -----------------------------
# Profile ordering + caps
# -----------------------------
prof_levels <- c("strict","moderate","relaxed")
caps <- c(10, 20, 30)

# ============================================================
# 1) Make hapmat safe for ape/pegas
#    (remove list-cols / enforce single-character A/C/G/T/N)
# ============================================================

fix_hapmat_listcols <- function(hm, missing_char = "N") {
  stopifnot("barcode" %in% names(hm))

  hm2 <- hm %>%
    dplyr::mutate(barcode = as.character(barcode)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      dplyr::across(-barcode, \(x) {
        if (is.list(x)) {
          purrr::map_chr(x, \(v) {
            v <- as.character(v)
            v <- v[!is.na(v)]
            if (length(v) == 0) NA_character_ else v[1]
          })
        } else {
          as.character(x)
        }
      })
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      dplyr::across(-barcode, \(x) {
        x[is.na(x)] <- missing_char
        x <- toupper(x)
        x[!x %in% c("A","C","G","T","N")] <- missing_char
        x
      })
    )

  hm2
}

# Apply to all profiles 
profile_results <- purrr::imap(profile_results, \(res, prof) {
  res$hapmat <- fix_hapmat_listcols(res$hapmat, missing_char = "N")
  res
})

# QC
hm <- profile_results$strict$hapmat
stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(names(hm)[names(hm) != "barcode"]))
stopifnot(!any(purrr::map_lgl(hm, is.list)))
tmp <- hm %>% dplyr::select(-barcode) %>% dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), as.character)) %>% as.matrix()
bad_vals <- unique(tmp[!is.na(tmp) & nchar(tmp) != 1])
if (length(bad_vals) > 0) {
  message("WARNING: Found non-1-char alleles (first few): ", paste(head(bad_vals, 10), collapse = ", "))
}

# ============================================================
# 2) Pegas haplotypes from hapmat (per profile)
#    - creates hap_per_profile (dna + haplotype object + barcode assignments)
#    - creates hap_table (barcode-level haplotype membership)
# ============================================================

# Unique lookup for sample_type 
bc_type <- qc_current %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    profile     = as.character(profile),
    barcode     = as.character(barcode),
    sample_type = as.character(sample_type)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(profile, barcode, sample_type)

call_haps_from_hapmat <- function(hapmat) {
  stopifnot("barcode" %in% names(hapmat))

  dna <- hapmat %>%
    dplyr::mutate(barcode = as.character(barcode)) %>%
    tibble::column_to_rownames("barcode") %>%
    as.matrix() %>%
    ape::as.DNAbin()

  h <- pegas::haplotype(dna)
  idx <- attr(h, "index")
  hap_names <- rownames(h)

  hap_assign <- purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(idx), \(i) {
    tibble::tibble(
      barcode  = rownames(dna)[ idx[[i]] ],
      haplotype = hap_names[i]
    )
  })

  list(dna = dna, h = h, hap_assign = hap_assign)
}

hap_per_profile <- purrr::imap(profile_results, \(res, prof) {
  out <- call_haps_from_hapmat(res$hapmat)
  out$profile <- as.character(prof)
  out
})

hap_table <- purrr::imap_dfr(hap_per_profile, \(obj, prof) {
  obj$hap_assign %>% dplyr::mutate(profile = as.character(prof))
}) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(bc_type, by = c("profile","barcode"))

# ============================================================
# 3) Haplogroups by cap (hierarchical clustering on haplotypes)
#    - uses ape::dist.dna on the haplotype object (obj$h)
#    - picks smallest cut height giving <= cap groups
# ============================================================

haplogroups_by_cap <- function(h_obj, cap = 20) {
  d  <- ape::dist.dna(h_obj, model = "raw", pairwise.deletion = TRUE)
  hc <- stats::hclust(d, method = "average")

  grid <- seq(0.0005, 0.20, by = 0.0005)
  n_groups <- vapply(grid, \(h) length(unique(stats::cutree(hc, h = h))), integer(1))

  i <- which(n_groups <= cap)[1]
  if (is.na(i)) i <- length(grid)

  grp <- stats::cutree(hc, h = grid[i])

  map <- tibble::tibble(
    haplotype = names(grp),
    haplogroup = paste0("HG", grp)
  )

  list(map = map, n_groups = length(unique(grp)), h_used = grid[i])
}

# ============================================================
# 4) SUPPORTING FIGURE 2: cap (10/20/30) vs eDNA capture
#       capture = proportion of eDNA barcodes in haplogroups that contain tissue
# ============================================================

edna_capture_cap <- purrr::imap_dfr(hap_per_profile, \(obj, prof) {
  prof <- as.character(prof)

  purrr::map_dfr(caps, \(cap_now) {

    hg <- haplogroups_by_cap(obj$h, cap = cap_now)

    barcode_hg <- hap_table %>%
      dplyr::filter(profile == prof) %>%
      dplyr::left_join(hg$map, by = "haplotype")

    hg_flags <- barcode_hg %>%
      dplyr::group_by(haplogroup) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(has_tissue = any(sample_type == "tissue"), .groups = "drop")

    prop <- barcode_hg %>%
      dplyr::filter(sample_type == "eDNA") %>%
      dplyr::left_join(hg_flags, by = "haplogroup") %>%
      dplyr::summarise(prop = mean(has_tissue, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
      dplyr::pull(prop)

    tibble::tibble(profile = prof, cap = cap_now, prop_edna_in_tissue_hg = prop)
  })
}) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(profile = factor(profile, levels = prof_levels))


p_capture_vs_cap <- ggplot2::ggplot(edna_capture_cap,
                                   ggplot2::aes(x = cap, y = prop_edna_in_tissue_hg)) +
  ggplot2::geom_line() +
  ggplot2::geom_point() +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ profile, nrow = 1) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Haplogroup cap (max groups)",
    y = "Proportion of eDNA in tissue-containing haplogroups"
  )

p_capture_vs_cap

# ============================================================
# 5) Final haplogroup assignment at cap = 20
#    - outputs:
#       haplogroup_map_cap20 (haplotype -> haplogroup)
#       barcode_haps_hg_cap20 (barcode-level haplotype + haplogroup)
#       hap_membership_hg_cap20 (hap_table + haplogroup attached)
# ============================================================

cap_final <- 20

haplogroup_map_cap20 <- purrr::imap_dfr(hap_per_profile, \(obj, prof) {
  prof <- as.character(prof)
  hg <- haplogroups_by_cap(obj$h, cap = cap_final)

  hg$map %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      profile   = prof,
      haplotype = as.character(haplotype),
      haplogroup = as.character(haplogroup)
    )
})

barcode_haps_hg_cap20 <- hap_table %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    profile    = as.character(profile),
    barcode    = as.character(barcode),
    sample_type = as.character(sample_type),
    haplotype  = as.character(haplotype)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(haplogroup_map_cap20, by = c("profile","haplotype"))

# Check
barcode_haps_hg_cap20 %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n = dplyr::n(),
    n_missing_hg = sum(is.na(haplogroup)),
    .by = profile
  ) %>% print()

hap_membership_hg_cap20 <- hap_table %>%
  dplyr::mutate(profile = as.character(profile), barcode = as.character(barcode)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    barcode_haps_hg_cap20 %>% dplyr::select(profile, barcode, haplogroup),
    by = c("profile","barcode")
  )

# ============================================================
# 6) Quick counts (haplotypes + haplogroups per profile)
# ============================================================

hap_counts <- hap_table %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n_haplotypes = dplyr::n_distinct(haplotype), .by = profile)

hg_counts <- barcode_haps_hg_cap20 %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n_haplogroups = dplyr::n_distinct(haplogroup), .by = profile)

hap_counts
hg_counts

# ============================================================
# 7) Haplogroup concordance in true tissue–eDNA pairs
#     uses pairs_one and barcode_haps_hg_cap20
# ============================================================

stopifnot(all(c("profile","tissue_barcode","edna_barcode") %in% names(pairs_one)))

pair_hg <- pairs_one %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    tissue_barcode = as.character(tissue_barcode),
    edna_barcode   = as.character(edna_barcode),
    pair_id = if ("pair_id" %in% names(pairs_one)) as.character(pair_id) else paste(tissue_barcode, "vs", edna_barcode)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    barcode_haps_hg_cap20 %>%
      dplyr::transmute(profile = as.character(profile),
                       tissue_barcode = as.character(barcode),
                       tissue_hg = as.character(haplogroup)),
    by = c("profile","tissue_barcode")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    barcode_haps_hg_cap20 %>%
      dplyr::transmute(profile = as.character(profile),
                       edna_barcode = as.character(barcode),
                       edna_hg = as.character(haplogroup)),
    by = c("profile","edna_barcode")
  )

pair_hg %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_pairs = dplyr::n(),
    n_missing_tissue_hg = sum(is.na(tissue_hg)),
    n_missing_edna_hg   = sum(is.na(edna_hg)),
    .by = profile
  ) %>% print()

hg_concordance <- pair_hg %>%
  dplyr::mutate(match = !is.na(tissue_hg) & !is.na(edna_hg) & tissue_hg == edna_hg) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_pairs = dplyr::n(),
    n_scored = sum(!is.na(match)),
    concordance = mean(match, na.rm = TRUE),
    .by = profile
  )

hg_concordance

# ============================================================
# 8) QC
# ============================================================

hap_sizes <- barcode_haps_hg_cap20 %>%
  dplyr::count(profile, haplotype, sample_type, name = "n") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = sample_type, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(total = tissue + eDNA)

ggplot2::ggplot(hap_sizes, ggplot2::aes(x = total)) +
  ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ profile, nrow = 1) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::labs(x = "Barcodes per haplotype", y = "Number of haplotypes")

# ============================================================
# 9) Exploratory: ALT-position set overlap (Jaccard) within haplogroups
# ============================================================

barcode_alt_sets <- purrr::imap_dfr(profile_results, \(res, profile_name) {

  panel_pos <- res$tier3$panel %>%
    dplyr::select(CHROM, POS) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

  ref_by_pos <- res$snps %>%
    dplyr::semi_join(panel_pos, by = c("CHROM","POS")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(CHROM, POS) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(REF_base = dplyr::first(REF), .groups = "drop")

  res$hapmat %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -barcode, names_to = "POS", values_to = "base") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(POS = as.double(POS)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(ref_by_pos, by = "POS") %>%  
    dplyr::mutate(is_alt = !is.na(REF_base) & base != REF_base) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is_alt) %>%
    dplyr::distinct(barcode, POS) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(alt_pos = list(sort(unique(POS))), .by = barcode) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(profile = as.character(profile_name))
})

alt_with_groups <- barcode_haps_hg_cap20 %>%
  dplyr::select(profile, barcode, sample_type, haplogroup, haplotype) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(barcode_alt_sets, by = c("profile","barcode")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(alt_pos = tidyr::replace_na(alt_pos, list(numeric(0))))

overlap_stats <- function(a, b) {
  inter <- length(intersect(a, b))
  uni   <- length(union(a, b))
  tibble::tibble(
    overlap = inter,
    union   = uni,
    jaccard = ifelse(uni > 0, inter/uni, NA_real_)
  )
}

hg_overlap <- alt_with_groups %>%
  dplyr::group_by(profile, haplogroup) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    tissue_sets = list(alt_pos[sample_type == "tissue"]),
    edna_sets   = list(alt_pos[sample_type == "eDNA"]),
    n_tissue    = sum(sample_type == "tissue"),
    n_eDNA      = sum(sample_type == "eDNA"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    pair_stats = purrr::pmap(list(tissue_sets, edna_sets), \(tsets, esets) {
      if (length(tsets) == 0 || length(esets) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())
      purrr::map_dfr(tsets, \(t) purrr::map_dfr(esets, \(e) overlap_stats(t, e)))
    })
  ) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(pair_stats) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(profile, haplogroup, n_tissue, n_eDNA) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_overlap   = mean(overlap, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_jaccard   = mean(jaccard, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_overlap = median(overlap, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

hg_overlap %>%
  dplyr::arrange(profile, desc(mean_jaccard)) %>%
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Pairwise SNP distances from hapmat then extract paired distances
#
# Required objects:
#   profile_results 
#   paired_samples    
# ============================================================

compute_dist_matrix <- function(hapmat) {

  hm <- as.data.frame(hapmat)

  # Ensure barcode column exists 
  if (!("barcode" %in% names(hm))) {
    rn <- rownames(hm)
    if (!is.null(rn) && any(grepl("^barcode", rn))) {
      hm <- hm %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("barcode")
    } else {
      names(hm)[1] <- "barcode"
    }
  }

  dna <- hm %>%
    dplyr::select(-barcode) %>%
    as.matrix() %>%
    ape::as.DNAbin()

  rownames(dna) <- hm$barcode

  d <- ape::dist.dna(dna, model = "raw", pairwise.deletion = FALSE)

  as.matrix(d)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Compute proportional distance matrices (0–1 scale)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
dist_list <- purrr::imap(profile_results, \(res, profile_name) {
  list(
    profile = profile_name,
    dist    = compute_dist_matrix(res$hapmat)
  )
})

# Check
names(profile_results$strict$hapmat)
head(profile_results$strict$hapmat[, 1:5])

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Convert proportional distances -> SNP mismatch counts
# ------------------------------------------------------------
dist_counts_list <- purrr::imap(profile_results, \(res, profile_name) {

  hapmat  <- as.data.frame(res$hapmat)
  n_sites <- ncol(hapmat) - 1  # exclude barcode column

  d_prop  <- dist_list[[profile_name]]$dist

  d_prop * n_sites
})

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Safe distance lookup
# ------------------------------------------------------------
safe_lookup <- function(dmat, a, b) {

  a <- as.character(a)
  b <- as.character(b)

  if (!a %in% rownames(dmat)) return(NA_real_)
  if (!b %in% colnames(dmat)) return(NA_real_)

  as.numeric(dmat[a, b])
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract distances for paired eDNA/tissue samples
# ------------------------------------------------------------
paired_valid <- paired_samples %>%
  mutate(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    barcode = as.character(barcode),
    sample_pair = na_if(trimws(as.character(sample_pair)), "NA"),
    pair_exists_in_profile = if_else(is.na(pair_sample_type), FALSE, TRUE),
    haplogroup_match = !is.na(pair_haplogroup) & haplogroup == pair_haplogroup,
    haplotype_match  = !is.na(pair_haplotype)  & haplotype  == pair_haplotype
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sample_pair))

paired_dist <- paired_valid %>%
  mutate(
    snp_distance = purrr::pmap_dbl(
      list(profile, barcode, sample_pair),
      \(profile, barcode, sample_pair) {

        dmat <- dist_counts_list[[as.character(profile)]]

        safe_lookup(dmat, barcode, sample_pair)

      }
    )
  )

# Check
paired_dist %>%
  filter(is.na(snp_distance)) %>%
  count(profile)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Histogram of eDNA/tissue SNP distances
# ------------------------------------------------------------
paired_dist_edna <- paired_dist %>%
  filter(sample_type == "eDNA", !is.na(snp_distance))

ggplot(paired_dist_edna, aes(x = snp_distance)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ profile) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "SNP differences (eDNA vs paired tissue)",
    y = "Number of pairs"
  )

# Table of distances
paired_dist %>%
  filter(sample_type == "eDNA") %>%
  select(profile, barcode, sample_pair, snp_distance)

names(qc_current)
names(paired_valid)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUPPORTING FIGURE 7
# Pairwise concordance across callable SNPs for each matched tissue–eDNA pair
#     Concordance defined as: 1 - (mismatches / called SNPs)
#
# Required object:
#   conc_plot with columns: profile, pair_id, status
# status values used here:
#   "match", "mismatch"  (rows with "no eDNA call" are excluded)
# ============================================================

pair_summary <- conc_plot %>%
  filter(status %in% c("match", "mismatch")) %>%   # callable comparisons only
  group_by(profile, pair_id) %>%
  summarise(
    n_called    = n(),
    n_mismatch  = sum(status == "mismatch"),
    concordance = 1 - (n_mismatch / n_called),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    class = case_when(
      concordance >= 0.95 ~ "high-confidence match",
      concordance <= 0.20 ~ "non-match candidate",
      TRUE                ~ "ambiguous"
    )
  )

p_pair_conc <- pair_summary %>%
  group_by(profile) %>%
  mutate(pair_id_ord = forcats::fct_reorder(pair_id, concordance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = concordance, y = forcats::fct_rev(pair_id_ord), shape = class)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ profile, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Concordance (1 - mismatches / called SNPs)",
    y = "Tissue ↔ eDNA pair",
    shape = NULL
  )

p_pair_conc
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE 6: SNP-level concordance tiles for paired eDNA–tissue samples
# Required object:
#   conc_plot
# ============================================================

base_tile <- function(df, prof) {

  df_prof <- df %>% dplyr::filter(profile == prof)
  stopifnot(nrow(df_prof) > 0)

  # Order pairs by mismatch rate 
  pair_levels <- df_prof %>%
    group_by(pair_id) %>%
    summarise(
      mismatch_rate = mean(status == "mismatch", na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(mismatch_rate) %>%
    pull(pair_id)

  df_prof <- df_prof %>%
    mutate(pair_id = factor(pair_id, levels = pair_levels)) %>%
    mutate(pair_id = forcats::fct_rev(pair_id))

  ggplot(df_prof, aes(x = snp_index, y = pair_id, fill = status)) +
    geom_tile(height = 0.9) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        "match"       = "#5E3C99",
        "mismatch"    = "#E6E6E6",
        "no eDNA call" = "red"
      )
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6)
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "SNP index",
      y = "Tissue/eDNA pair",
      fill = NULL
    )
}

p_strict   <- base_tile(conc_plot, "strict")
p_moderate <- base_tile(conc_plot, "moderate")
p_relaxed  <- base_tile(conc_plot, "relaxed")

p_strict
p_moderate
p_relaxed

```
```{r}
# Fix data formatting for subsequent analyses 
paired_samples <- paired_samples %>% mutate(sample_type = tolower(sample_type))
qc_current     <- qc_current     %>% mutate(sample_type = tolower(sample_type))
depth_long     <- depth_long     %>% mutate(sample_type = tolower(sample_type))
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# Paired eDNA–tissue SNP metrics computed directly from alleles_panel
#     SNP distance and mismatch rate on callable positions
#
# Required objects:
#   qc_current
#   alleles_panel
# ============================================================
true_pairs <- paired_samples %>%
  mutate(
    profile     = as.character(profile),
    barcode     = as.character(barcode),
    sample_pair = na_if(as.character(sample_pair), "NA")
  ) %>%
  filter(sample_type == "edna", !is.na(sample_pair)) %>%   # <-- change
  transmute(profile, edna_barcode = barcode, tissue_barcode = sample_pair) %>%
  distinct()

panel_pos <- alleles_panel %>%
  transmute(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    POS     = as.double(POS)
  ) %>%
  distinct(profile, POS)

ap <- alleles_panel %>%
  transmute(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    barcode = as.character(barcode),
    POS     = as.double(POS),
    allele  = as.character(allele)
  )

# Expand each true pair to all panel SNP positions
pair_pos <- true_pairs %>%
  inner_join(panel_pos, by = "profile")

pair_alleles <- pair_pos %>%
  left_join(
    ap %>% rename(edna_barcode = barcode, allele_edna = allele),
    by = c("profile", "edna_barcode", "POS")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    ap %>% rename(tissue_barcode = barcode, allele_tissue = allele),
    by = c("profile", "tissue_barcode", "POS")
  )

missing <- c(NA, "N", "-", "?")

pair_snp_metrics <- pair_alleles %>%
  mutate(
    callable = !(allele_edna %in% missing | allele_tissue %in% missing),
    mismatch = callable & (allele_edna != allele_tissue)
  ) %>%
  group_by(profile, edna_barcode, tissue_barcode) %>%
  summarise(
    n_comp = sum(callable, na.rm = TRUE),
    snp_distance = sum(mismatch, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_mismatch_callable = if_else(n_comp > 0, snp_distance / n_comp, NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Profile-level summary 
pair_snp_metrics %>%
  group_by(profile) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs          = n(),
    n_exact          = sum(snp_distance == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_one_snp        = sum(snp_distance == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_snp_diff    = mean(snp_distance, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_prop_mismatch = mean(prop_mismatch_callable, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

pair_snp_metrics %>%
  group_by(profile) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs    = n(),
    n_zero_comp = sum(n_comp == 0),
    min_comp   = min(n_comp),
    median_comp = median(n_comp),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUPPORTING FIGURE 8
# Depth profiles across defining SNP positions, grouped by haplogroup
# - Facets are haplogroups that contain at least one eDNA barcode
# - Within each haplogroup facet: barcodes are ordered by mean log-depth
# - Left side shows barcode labels + a sample-type color strip (tissue vs eDNA)
# - Heatmap shows log10(DP+1) across SNP index (panel positions for that profile)
#
# Required objects:
#   conc_plot 
#   depth_long 
#   qc_current 
# ============================================================

library(ggnewscale)
library(viridis)

plot_depth_edna_haplogroups_collapsed <- function(
  prof,
  conc_plot,
  depth_long,
  qc_current,
  col_edna = "#7B3294",
  col_tissue = "#7A7A7A",
  label_x = -6,
  strip_x  = -2.5,
  strip_width = 2.8,
  singleton_label = "eDNA-only singletons"
) {

  # SNP positions used in the concordance plot for this profile
  pos_set <- conc_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(profile == prof) %>%
    dplyr::distinct(POS) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(POS = as.numeric(POS))

  stopifnot(nrow(pos_set) > 0)

  # Metadata for barcodes in this profile
  meta_prof <- paired_samples %>%
    dplyr::filter(profile == prof, sample_type %in% c("tissue","edna")) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      barcode     = as.character(barcode),
      sample_type = as.character(sample_type),
      haplogroup  = as.character(haplogroup)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(haplogroup)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

  stopifnot(nrow(meta_prof) > 0)

  # Keep haplogroups that have at least one eDNA member
  hg_keep <- meta_prof %>%
    dplyr::group_by(haplogroup) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(has_edna = any(sample_type == "edna"), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::filter(has_edna) %>%
    dplyr::pull(haplogroup)

  meta_prof <- meta_prof %>% dplyr::filter(haplogroup %in% hg_keep)

  # Haplogroup metadata for facet labels and ordering
  hg_meta <- meta_prof %>%
    dplyr::group_by(haplogroup) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      n_total  = dplyr::n_distinct(barcode),
      n_edna   = sum(sample_type == "edna"),
      n_tissue = sum(sample_type == "tissue"),
      is_mixed = (n_edna > 0 & n_tissue > 0),
      is_edna_only_singleton = (n_edna > 0 & n_tissue == 0 & n_total == 1),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Depth data restricted to SNP positions used for  profile
  depth_prof <- depth_long %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      barcode = as.character(barcode),
      POS     = as.numeric(POS),
      DP      = as.numeric(DP)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::semi_join(pos_set, by = "POS")

  stopifnot(nrow(depth_prof) > 0)

  plot_df <- depth_prof %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(meta_prof, by = "barcode") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(depth_log = log10(DP + 1))

  if (nrow(plot_df) == 0) {
    message(
      "No depth rows after joining for profile = ", prof,
      ". (Depth table missing these barcodes.)"
    )
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Map POS to an index (x-axis = snp_index)
  pos_index <- plot_df %>%
    dplyr::distinct(POS) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(POS) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(snp_index = dplyr::row_number())

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    dplyr::left_join(pos_index, by = "POS") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(hg_meta, by = "haplogroup") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      hg_lab = ifelse(
        is_edna_only_singleton,
        singleton_label,
        paste0(haplogroup, " (n=", n_total, "; edna=", n_edna, ")")
      )
    )

  # Facet ordering: mixed first, then by eDNA count, then total count
  facet_levels <- hg_meta %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      hg_lab = ifelse(
        is_edna_only_singleton,
        singleton_label,
        paste0(haplogroup, " (n=", n_total, "; edna=", n_edna, ")")
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(is_mixed), desc(n_edna), desc(n_total), haplogroup) %>%
    dplyr::pull(hg_lab) %>%
    unique()

  facet_levels <- c(setdiff(facet_levels, singleton_label), singleton_label)

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(hg_lab = factor(hg_lab, levels = facet_levels))

  # Barcode ordering within each haplogroup facet: tissue then eDNA
```
