```{r}
  barcode_levels <- plot_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(hg_lab, barcode, sample_type) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(mean_depth = mean(depth_log, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(
      hg_lab,
      factor(sample_type, levels = c("tissue","edna")),
      desc(mean_depth)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::pull(barcode) %>%
    unique()

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(barcode = factor(barcode, levels = barcode_levels))

  # left-side label strip
  strip_df <- plot_df %>%
    dplyr::distinct(hg_lab, barcode, sample_type) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(x = strip_x)

  label_df <- strip_df %>%
    dplyr::distinct(hg_lab, barcode, sample_type) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(x = label_x)

  ggplot() +
    geom_text(
      data = label_df,
      aes(x = x, y = barcode, label = as.character(barcode), color = sample_type),
      hjust = 1, size = 3, fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(tissue = col_tissue, edna = col_edna), guide = "none") +
    ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
    geom_tile(
      data = strip_df,
      aes(x = x, y = barcode, fill = sample_type),
      width = strip_width, height = 0.9,
      color = "white", linewidth = 0.2
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(tissue = col_tissue, edna = col_edna), name = "Sample type") +
    ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
    geom_tile(
      data = plot_df,
      aes(x = snp_index, y = barcode, fill = depth_log),
      height = 0.9
    ) +
    facet_grid(hg_lab ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Depth\nlog10(dp+1)", option = "magma") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(label_x - 1, NA), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      strip.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5.5, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 80)
    ) +
    labs(x = "SNP index", y = "Barcode")
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Supporting Fig 8 panels
# ------------------------------------------------------------
p_strict  <- plot_depth_edna_haplogroups_collapsed("strict",   conc_plot, depth_long, paired_valid)
p_mod     <- plot_depth_edna_haplogroups_collapsed("moderate", conc_plot, depth_long, paired_valid)
p_relaxed <- plot_depth_edna_haplogroups_collapsed("relaxed",  conc_plot, depth_long, paired_valid)

# increase y-axis title margin
add_y_title_margin <- function(p, r = 50) {
  p + theme(axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = r)))
}

p_strict  <- add_y_title_margin(p_strict,  r = 50)
p_mod     <- add_y_title_margin(p_mod,     r = 50)
p_relaxed <- add_y_title_margin(p_relaxed, r = 50)

p_strict
p_mod
p_relaxed
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Permutation test: are true eDNA–tissue pairs more similar than random pairing?
#     Uses a distance matrix of mismatch proportions computed from hapmat
#
# Required objects:
#   profile_results
#   paired_valid
#   qc_current
# ============================================================

paired_valid <- paired_valid %>% mutate(sample_type = tolower(sample_type))
qc_current   <- qc_current   %>% mutate(sample_type = tolower(sample_type))

pairs_edna <- paired_valid %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    profile     = as.character(profile),
    edna_bc     = as.character(barcode),
    tissue_bc   = as.character(sample_pair),
    sample_type = tolower(as.character(sample_type))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(sample_type == "edna", !is.na(tissue_bc)) %>%
  dplyr::select(profile, edna_bc, tissue_bc) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

glimpse(pairs_edna)
paired_valid_edna <- paired_valid %>%
  filter(sample_type == "edna", !is.na(sample_pair)) %>%
  transmute(
    profile = as.character(profile),
    edna_bc = as.character(barcode),
    tissue_bc = as.character(sample_pair)
  ) %>%
  distinct()

for (prof in names(dist_counts_list)) {

  dmat <- dist_counts_list[[prof]]

  cat("\nPROFILE:", prof, "\n")

  print(
    pairs_edna %>%
      dplyr::filter(profile == prof) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(
        n_pairs_raw   = dplyr::n(),
        edna_in_mat   = sum(edna_bc   %in% rownames(dmat)),
        tissue_in_mat = sum(tissue_bc %in% colnames(dmat))
      )
  )
}

# Check
print(names(dist_counts_list))
print(sort(unique(pairs_edna$profile)))


panel_sizes <- paired_valid %>%
  distinct(profile, n_panel_sites) %>%
  arrange(profile)
panel_sizes
panel_lookup <- setNames(panel_sizes$n_panel_sites, panel_sizes$profile)

diag_tbl <- purrr::imap_dfr(dist_counts_list, \(dmat, prof) {

  tmp <- pairs_edna[pairs_edna$profile == prof, , drop = FALSE]

  tibble::tibble(
    profile       = prof,
    n_pairs_raw   = nrow(tmp),
    edna_in_mat   = sum(tmp$edna_bc   %in% rownames(dmat)),
    tissue_in_mat = sum(tmp$tissue_bc %in% colnames(dmat))
  )
})

perm_test_from_distcounts <- function(profile_value,
                                      pairs_edna,
                                      qc_current,
                                      dist_counts_list,
                                      n_perm = 10000,
                                      seed = 123,
                                      verbose = TRUE) {

  set.seed(seed)

  prof <- tolower(trimws(as.character(profile_value)[1]))
  dmat <- dist_counts_list[[prof]]
  if (is.null(dmat)) stop("No distance matrix found for profile: ", prof)

    pairs_edna2 <- pairs_edna
  names(pairs_edna2) <- tolower(names(pairs_edna2))

  dat <- data.frame(
    profile   = tolower(trimws(as.character(pairs_edna2[["profile"]]))),
    edna_bc   = trimws(as.character(pairs_edna2[["edna_bc"]])),
    tissue_bc = trimws(as.character(pairs_edna2[["tissue_bc"]])),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  dat <- dat[dat$profile == prof, c("edna_bc","tissue_bc"), drop = FALSE]
  dat <- unique(dat)

  # Keep only those that exist in matrix
  dat <- dat[dat$edna_bc %in% rownames(dmat) & dat$tissue_bc %in% colnames(dmat), , drop = FALSE]
  n_pairs <- nrow(dat)

  qc2 <- qc_current
  names(qc2) <- tolower(names(qc2))

  qc2$profile     <- tolower(trimws(as.character(qc2[["profile"]])))
  qc2$barcode     <- trimws(as.character(qc2[["barcode"]]))
  qc2$sample_type <- tolower(trimws(as.character(qc2[["sample_type"]])))

  tissue_pool <- unique(qc2$barcode[qc2$profile == prof & qc2$sample_type == "tissue"])
  tissue_pool <- intersect(tissue_pool, colnames(dmat))

  if (verbose) {
    cat("\nPROFILE:", prof,
        "\n  n_pairs:", n_pairs,
        "\n  tissue_pool:", length(tissue_pool),
        "\n")
  }

  if (n_pairs == 0 || length(tissue_pool) == 0) {
    return(list(profile = prof, n_pairs = n_pairs, obs_mean = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_,
                perm_means = numeric(), tissue_pool_n = length(tissue_pool)))
  }

  # Observed mean distance
  panel_size <- panel_lookup[[prof]]
  obs <- mapply(function(a,b) dmat[a,b], dat$edna_bc, dat$tissue_bc) / panel_size
  obs_mean <- mean(obs, na.rm = TRUE)

  # Null distribution
  replace_t <- length(tissue_pool) < n_pairs
  perm_means <- replicate(n_perm, {
    rand_t <- sample(tissue_pool, n_pairs, replace = replace_t)
    d <- mapply(function(a,b) dmat[a,b], dat$edna_bc, rand_t) / panel_size
    mean(d, na.rm = TRUE)
  })

  p <- mean(perm_means <= obs_mean)

  list(profile = prof, n_pairs = n_pairs, obs_mean = obs_mean, p_value = p,
       perm_means = perm_means, tissue_pool_n = length(tissue_pool))
}

profile_names <- names(dist_counts_list)

perm_out <- purrr::map(profile_names, \(prof)
  perm_test_from_distcounts(
    profile_value = prof,
    pairs_edna = pairs_edna,
    qc_current = qc_current,
    dist_counts_list = dist_counts_list,
    n_perm = 10000
  )
)
results_perm <- purrr::map_dfr(perm_out, \(x)
  tibble::tibble(
    profile = x$profile,
    n_pairs = x$n_pairs,
    obs_mean = x$obs_mean,
    p_value = x$p_value
  )
)

results_perm

```

```{r}
# Long format results for plotting
perm_results <- purrr::imap_dfr(perm_out, \(x, prof) {
  tibble(
    profile = x$profile,
    perm_mean = x$perm_means,
    obs_mean = x$obs_mean
  )
})

# Plot histogram + observed line (Supporting Fig 9 / permutation panel)
ggplot(perm_results, aes(x = perm_mean)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = obs_mean), linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ profile, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Mean callable mismatch rate under random pairing",
    y = "Permutation count",
  )

```
